signals.reasoning: Cover the live ARB short immediately. The trade has gone from a winner to a significant live loss, the warning is urgent, and the board is still strongly bullish against the short thesis.
BTC is constructive, but the active veto keeps benchmark exposure in watch mode rather than action mode.
ETH has some constructive tone, but not enough to justify a fresh trade under the veto regime.
SOL is too mixed to justify action while the event brake is active.
STRK has some improving squeeze traits, but the setup is not strong enough to overcome the veto state.
BLAST has an interesting squeeze profile on paper, but the trade quality is too poor to trust under veto conditions.
HEMI has some constructive tilt, but not enough clean edge to justify action here.
INIT is a reasonable watch-only long because mechanics are constructive and funding is mildly supportive, but veto keeps it sidelined for now.
FOGO remains a valid bearish lean because the tape is still weak even though funding has started to improve slightly.
MAVIA remains a clean bearish fade because funding is extremely positive, crowding is high, and the market quality is still weak.
DASH remains one of the best long candidates on raw mechanics, but veto should keep it in watch mode rather than as a fresh buy.
